questions.question: 中文导读：What does 'entropy' measure in information theory?
options: 中文选项：The energy of a physical system
中文选项：The average surprise or uncertainty in a probability distribution
中文选项：The number of bits in a binary message
中文选项：The error rate of a communication channel
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Entropy H(P) = -sum(p(x) * log(p(x))) measures the average amount of surprise across all outcomes. High entropy means high uncertainty (uniform distribution). Low entropy means the outcome is predictable.

questions.question: 中文导读：What is the cross-entropy loss commonly used for in neural networks?
options: 中文选项：Regression tasks with continuous outputs
中文选项：Classification tasks, measuring how far predicted probabilities are from true labels
中文选项：Generating new data samples
中文选项：Regularizing model weights to prevent overfitting
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Cross-entropy loss H(P,Q) = -sum(p(x)*log(q(x))) measures the difference between the true distribution (labels) and the model's predicted distribution. It is THE standard loss for classification.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why is minimizing cross-entropy equivalent to minimizing KL divergence during training?
options: 中文选项：Because KL divergence is always zero during training
中文选项：Because cross-entropy = entropy + KL divergence, and the entropy of the true labels is constant, so minimizing cross-entropy minimizes KL divergence
中文选项：Because cross-entropy and KL divergence are the same formula
中文选项：Because the model's entropy equals zero at convergence
questions.explanation: 中文解析：H(P,Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P||Q). Since the true distribution P doesn't change during training, H(P) is constant. Minimizing H(P,Q) is the same as minimizing D_KL(P||Q) -- pushing the model toward the true distribution.

questions.question: 中文导读：A language model has perplexity 50 on a test set. What does this mean?
options: 中文选项：The model makes 50 errors per sentence
中文选项：On average, the model is as uncertain as if it were choosing uniformly from 50 possible next tokens at each step
中文选项：The model has 50 layers
中文选项：The model was trained for 50 epochs
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Perplexity = e^(cross-entropy). A perplexity of 50 means the model's uncertainty at each token is equivalent to picking randomly from 50 equally likely options. Lower perplexity means better predictions.

questions.question: 中文导读：How does mutual information differ from Pearson correlation for feature selection?
options: 中文选项：Mutual information is faster to compute
中文选项：Mutual information detects any statistical dependency (linear or nonlinear) while correlation only detects linear relationships
中文选项：Pearson correlation works for any data type while mutual information only works for continuous data
中文选项：They always give the same feature rankings
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Mutual information I(X;Y) captures all statistical dependencies between variables, including nonlinear and non-monotonic ones. Pearson correlation only measures linear association, missing many important relationships.
